ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p (TA505/FIN11) LEMURLOOT post-exploitation: w3wp.exe spawning recon/SQL tooling on MOVEit & GoAnywhere MFT hosts
ucs.description: Hunts for the Cl0p signature post-exploit chain on managed-file-transfer servers — IIS worker (w3wp.exe) running the MOVEit Transfer or GoAnywhere MFT app pool spawning cmd/powershell/sqlcmd/bcp/whoami. This is the exact behavior CISA AA23-158A and Mandiant attribute to Cl0p mass-exploitation of CVE-2023-34362 (MOVEit) and CVE-2023-0669 (GoAnywhere), including LEMURLOOT (human2.aspx) staging, AD recon and DB enumeration before exfil.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as child_cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdlines from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitTransfer*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MOVEit.DMZ*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitDMZ*" OR Processes.parent_process="*GoAnywhere*" OR Processes.parent_process="*goanywhere*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","sqlcmd.exe","bcp.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","reg.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort 0 - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p / TA505 / FIN11 — MOVEit & GoAnywhere LEMURLOOT post-exploit chain
let SuspectChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","sqlcmd.exe","bcp.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","wmic.exe","reg.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
// Scope to MOVEit Transfer / DMZ / GoAnywhere MFT app pools — Cl0p targets
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MOVEitTransfer","MOVEit.DMZ","MOVEitDMZ","GoAnywhere","goanywhere","SilverlightControlsAppPool")
| where FileName in~ (SuspectChildren)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, ProcessIntegrityLevel
// Pivot to the LEMURLOOT artefact write (human2.aspx) on the same host within ±1h
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("human2.aspx","_human2.aspx","human2.aspx.lnk")
         or (FolderPath has_any (@"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\", @"\GoAnywhere\") and FileName endswith ".aspx" and FileName !~ "human.aspx")
    | project WebShellTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, WebShellFile = FileName, WebShellPath = FolderPath, WebShellSHA256 = SHA256
  ) on DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p / TA505 / FIN11 — MOVEit & GoAnywhere LEMURLOOT post-exploit chain (Sentinel)
// Note: 4688 ParentProcessCommandLine is unavailable, so we filter by parent image + Internet-facing scope.
let IFHosts = DeviceInfo
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | where IsInternetFacing == true
    | summarize by Computer = DeviceName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (@"\cmd.exe", @"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe", @"\sqlcmd.exe", @"\bcp.exe", @"\whoami.exe", @"\net.exe", @"\net1.exe", @"\nltest.exe", @"\systeminfo.exe", @"\wmic.exe", @"\reg.exe")
| where Computer in (IFHosts) or Computer matches regex @"(?i)(MOVEIT|MFT|GOANYWHERE|XFER|TRANSFER)"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, ProcessId, NewProcessId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p MOVEit/GoAnywhere LEMURLOOT Web Shell Post-Exploitation
id: a4f7c1ce-e0c2-4dae-9f1b-cl0p-movetit-2023
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious child processes of IIS w3wp.exe running under the MOVEit Transfer or GoAnywhere MFT application pools. This is the LEMURLOOT/human2.aspx post-exploitation behaviour Cl0p (TA505/FIN11/GRACEFUL SPIDER) used to mass-exploit CVE-2023-34362 (MOVEit) and CVE-2023-0669 (GoAnywhere).
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-158a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/zero-day-moveit-data-theft
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2024/01/01
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - threat.actor.cl0p
  - threat.actor.fin11
  - threat.actor.ta505
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MOVEitTransfer'
      - 'MOVEit.DMZ'
      - 'MOVEitDMZ'
      - 'GoAnywhere'
      - 'goanywhere'
      - 'SilverlightControlsAppPool'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\sqlcmd.exe'
      - '\bcp.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
falsepositives:
  - Vendor-supplied MOVEit/GoAnywhere maintenance scripts that legitimately invoke sqlcmd/bcp from the IIS app pool — baseline by hash and parent path before alerting.
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is THE Cl0p signature TTP from 2021-2023 (Accellion FTA → SolarWinds Serv-U → GoAnywhere → MOVEit). Cl0p does not deliver via spearphish-and-payload like most ransomware crews — they mass-exploit zero-days in internet-facing file-transfer appliances, drop a small ASPX web shell (LEMURLOOT/human2.aspx for MOVEit), and immediately use the IIS worker process to run AD recon (whoami/net/nltest) a
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p ransomware impact chain: shadow-copy/recovery destruction burst + Cl0p-branded ransom note or extension drop
ucs.description: Hunts for the final Cl0p impact stage — a short-window burst of shadow-copy destruction (vssadmin/wmic), boot-config recovery disablement (bcdedit) and backup-catalog deletion (wbadmin) followed (or accompanied) by mass file renames to .clop/.Cllp/.C_L_O_P/.C1op or the drop of the Cl0p-branded ransom note (README_README.txt / Cl0pReadMe.txt / !_README_!.txt). This is the documented pre-encryption + encryption fingerprint of the Cl0p locker family used by TA505/FIN11.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" Processes.process="*delete*" Processes.process="*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" Processes.process="*shadowcopy*" Processes.process="*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" (Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*No*" OR Processes.process="*ignoreallfailures*")) OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" Processes.process="*delete*" Processes.process="*catalog*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process _time
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval bucket=floor(_time/900)
| stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime dc(process_name) as distinct_actions values(process_name) as actions values(process) as cmdlines by dest user bucket
| where distinct_actions >= 2
| join type=left dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name IN ("README_README.txt","_README_.txt","Cl0pReadMe.txt","ClopReadMe.txt","!_README_!.txt","HOW_TO_RECOVER_FILES.txt") OR Filesystem.file_name="*.clop" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.Cllp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.C_L_O_P" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.C1op") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | stats sum(count) as cl0p_artifact_count values(file_name) as cl0p_artifacts by dest
  ]
| where distinct_actions >= 2 OR cl0p_artifact_count > 5
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
| sort 0 - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p (TA505/FIN11) ransomware impact chain — recovery destruction + Cl0p-branded artefact drop
let WindowMin = 15m;
let Cl0pNotes = dynamic(["README_README.txt","_README_.txt","Cl0pReadMe.txt","ClopReadMe.txt","!_README_!.txt","HOW_TO_RECOVER_FILES.txt","!CL0P_README.TXT"]);
let RecoveryAttacks = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | extend Action = case(
        FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadows", "VSS_Delete",
        FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "shadowcopy" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete", "WMIC_Shadow_Delete",
        FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled" or ProcessCommandLine has "ignoreallfailures"), "BCD_Disable_Recovery",
        FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete" and ProcessCommandLine has "catalog", "Wbadmin_Delete_Catalog",
        FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy"), "PS_Shadow_Delete",
        "")
    | where Action != ""
    | summarize Actions = make_set(Action),
                ActionCount = dcount(Action),
                FirstAction = min(Timestamp),
                LastAction = max(Timestamp),
                CmdLines = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 20)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, WindowMin)
    | where ActionCount >= 2;     // multiple distinct destruction primitives in one window — ransomware-shaped
let Cl0pArtefacts = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where FileName in~ (Cl0pNotes)
         or FileName endswith ".clop" or FileName endswith ".Cllp"
         or FileName endswith ".C_L_O_P" or FileName endswith ".C1op"
         or FileName endswith ".Clop"
    | summarize Cl0pArtefactCount = count(),
                Cl0pSampleFiles = make_set(FileName, 20),
                Cl0pFirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
                ProcessName = any(InitiatingProcessFileName),
                ProcessCmd = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine)
                by DeviceId, DeviceName;
// Either the destruction-burst alone is high-fidelity, OR pair it with the Cl0p artefact
RecoveryAttacks
| join kind=leftouter Cl0pArtefacts on DeviceId
| extend Cl0pConfidence = case(ActionCount >= 3, "high",
                               isnotempty(Cl0pSampleFiles) and Cl0pArtefactCount > 5, "high",
                               ActionCount >= 2, "medium",
                               "low")
| project FirstAction, LastAction, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ActionCount, Actions, CmdLines,
          Cl0pArtefactCount, Cl0pSampleFiles, Cl0pFirstSeen, ProcessName, ProcessCmd,
          Cl0pConfidence
| order by FirstAction desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p impact chain (Sentinel) — recovery destruction + Cl0p artefact via SecurityEvent + ImFileEvent
let WindowMin = 15m;
let Cl0pNotes = dynamic(["README_README.txt","_README_.txt","Cl0pReadMe.txt","ClopReadMe.txt","!_README_!.txt","HOW_TO_RECOVER_FILES.txt","!CL0P_README.TXT"]);
let RecoveryAttacks = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Action = case(
        NewProcessName endswith @"\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "delete" and CommandLine has "shadows", "VSS_Delete",
        NewProcessName endswith @"\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has "shadowcopy" and CommandLine has "delete", "WMIC_Shadow_Delete",
        NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe" and (CommandLine has "recoveryenabled" or CommandLine has "ignoreallfailures"), "BCD_Disable_Recovery",
        NewProcessName endswith @"\wbadmin.exe" and CommandLine has "delete" and CommandLine has "catalog", "Wbadmin_Delete_Catalog",
        NewProcessName has_any (@"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy"), "PS_Shadow_Delete",
        "")
    | where Action != ""
    | summarize Actions = make_set(Action),
                ActionCount = dcount(Action),
                FirstAction = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastAction = max(TimeGenerated),
                CmdLines = make_set(CommandLine, 20)
                by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMin)
    | where ActionCount >= 2;
let Cl0pArtefacts = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where TargetFileName in~ (Cl0pNotes)
         or TargetFileName endswith ".clop" or TargetFileName endswith ".Cllp"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".C_L_O_P" or TargetFileName endswith ".C1op"
         or TargetFileName endswith ".Clop"
    | summarize Cl0pArtefactCount = count(),
                Cl0pSampleFiles = make_set(TargetFileName, 20),
                Cl0pFirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
                ProcessNames = make_set(ActingProcessName)
                by Computer = DvcHostname;
RecoveryAttacks
| join kind=leftouter Cl0pArtefacts on Computer
| extend Cl0pConfidence = case(ActionCount >= 3, "high",
                               isnotempty(Cl0pSampleFiles) and Cl0pArtefactCount > 5, "high",
                               ActionCount >= 2, "medium",
                               "low")
| project FirstAction, LastAction, Computer, Account,
          ActionCount, Actions, CmdLines,
          Cl0pArtefactCount, Cl0pSampleFiles, Cl0pFirstSeen, ProcessNames,
          Cl0pConfidence
| order by FirstAction desc
ucs.rationale: Cl0p's locker has a deterministic pre-encryption pattern documented across CISA advisories and IR reports: it issues vssadmin delete shadows, wmic shadowcopy delete, and bcdedit /set recoveryenabled No / ignoreallfailures yes back-to-back, then writes a ransom note titled README_README.txt (or variants) and renames victim files with .clop / .Cllp / .C_L_O_P / .C1op extensions. Detecting any TWO of
